title: glandularia macrosperma
intro: Glandularia macrosperma, also known as large-seeded verbena, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Glandularia macrosperma include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in tincture form to extract its medicinal properties.
